Alright, welcome back to Theme Plate 3. This is the way to early ultimatum. And sometimes the best way to get a commitment is for your prospect to see that they're not committed to the opposite. Now let me say that again, sometimes the best way to get commitment is for the prospect to see they're not committed to the opposite. So by leading with a counter-intuitive invitation to exclude themselves, to say, I don't want this, we get prospects to realize that they're in fact committed to the result and your pre-launch that delivers it. So that is what we are going to work on in the third and final invite of your webinar or launch final or whatever type of launch you're using. And this is really what I would call pulse point number one. It's really kind of where you check in as a conversion coach and just ask them, you know, like, based on what you've heard, like, you've seen a few messages. Are you feeling it? Like, is this something you want to continue talking about and you get that buy-in? And sometimes when, you know, people are faced with that reality where like, okay, no, this person's serious. They're giving me an out right now. And if they don't take that out, that really ramps up that consistency principle. You know, that's what really kind of shines that light back on them that, oh, wow, okay, no, I really do want to be here. I really do want to do this. So it's one of those, it's getting real kind of moments. And as a coach who cares about your prospect and the outcomes they want to achieve and being willing to challenge them, you know, to go after it, this is a very important email to include. So this is the way too early, ultimatum. It's going to break down like this. Since they've already seen your previous emails, you're just going to start off right away with a call to action with a little bit of urgency and then come out with a big outcome. The launch is promising. An invitation to opt out. This is the big one that a lot of marketers will get scared of. Why would I put up an invitation to opt out? Well, you know what? That would actually be a lot more telling than someone just ignoring and not taking actions. So just putting that in front of them, give them that invitation to opt out and frame in action, frame that in action. If they didn't opt out, you could frame that lack of opting out as implied belongingness. Like if they didn't opt out, it's because they should be here. They've decided to be here, reflect that back with your second call to action, close it with that exclusive empowerment once again and that optional PS to sell the event launch video, which at this point, you should already have the content for that based on your other theme plates. So that's it. The big point here is really the invitation for the opt out. If they're still there after that, you frame that in action as implied belongingness. You frame that choice not to opt out as a choice to participate. So that's what we're doing. Let's take a quick look at how this plays out. So example, should I cross you off the list?
